description:
Your team’s configuration-management daemon appends change events to the file

    /home/user/config_changes.log

Each event is a single, newline-terminated line that looks like the following template (all on one line, no leading or trailing blanks):

YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS CONFIG: <absolute_path> status=<STATE> checksum=<SHA1_or_SHA256_hex>

STATE can be one of: CREATED, MODIFIED, UNCHANGED, or DELETED (all uppercase).

For a quick audit you only need to look at files that were changed in place, i.e. whose status is exactly “MODIFIED”.  
Create a one-off extraction so that:

1. A directory “/home/user/filtered” exists (create it if it does not).
2. Inside that directory, write a new file named “modified_only.log”.
3. “modified_only.log” must contain **only** the original log lines whose `status=MODIFIED`
   appears anywhere in the line.
4. Preserve the original order of those lines; make no other changes to them.
5. Do not include a trailing blank line at the end of the file.

When you are done, the command

    cat /home/user/filtered/modified_only.log

must print exactly the two lines below and nothing else:

2024-01-15 10:12:04 CONFIG: /etc/ssh/sshd_config status=MODIFIED checksum=87acec17cd9dcd20a716cc2cf67417b71c8a7016
2024-01-15 10:12:05 CONFIG: /etc/nginx/nginx.conf status=MODIFIED checksum=3b5d5c3712955042212316173ccf37be

You may accomplish the task with a single, appropriately-constructed command that uses a regular expression (for example, `grep`, `egrep`, `sed`, or `awk`), but any solution that meets the five requirements above is acceptable.

Once the file has been created exactly as specified, your work is complete.
truth:
1. Before the task starts, the file /home/user/config_changes.log already exists with the following five lines (each terminated by a single newline, no extra blank lines):

2024-01-15 10:12:03 CONFIG: /etc/ssh/sshd_config status=UNCHANGED checksum=b1946ac92492d2347c6235b4d2611184
2024-01-15 10:12:04 CONFIG: /etc/ssh/sshd_config status=MODIFIED checksum=87acec17cd9dcd20a716cc2cf67417b71c8a7016
2024-01-15 10:12:05 CONFIG: /etc/nginx/nginx.conf status=MODIFIED checksum=3b5d5c3712955042212316173ccf37be
2024-01-15 10:12:06 CONFIG: /etc/passwd status=CREATED checksum=764efa883dda1e11db47671c4a3bbd9e
2024-01-15 10:12:07 CONFIG: /etc/nginx/nginx.conf status=UNCHANGED checksum=cb837b763cf375a3530d9cf68bbee585

2. After the agent completes, the directory /home/user/filtered must exist.

3. After completion, the file /home/user/filtered/modified_only.log must exist with **exactly** the following two lines (each terminated by one newline, no extra blank lines at the start or end):

2024-01-15 10:12:04 CONFIG: /etc/ssh/sshd_config status=MODIFIED checksum=87acec17cd9dcd20a716cc2cf67417b71c8a7016
2024-01-15 10:12:05 CONFIG: /etc/nginx/nginx.conf status=MODIFIED checksum=3b5d5c3712955042212316173ccf37be

4. The automated test will:

   a. Verify that /home/user/filtered exists and is a directory.  
   b. Verify that /home/user/filtered/modified_only.log exists and is a regular file.  
   c. Run “cat /home/user/filtered/modified_only.log” and compare the exact byte sequence (including newline placement) to the two-line block above.  
   d. Optionally check that the user did not modify /home/user/config_changes.log (its checksum must remain unchanged).

No other files or directories are required for verification.
name: task_000000_c66bc26b
